+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: de80
+project: ui-command-cleanup
+title: 'Bug: Host-driven nav regression ‚ÄĒ arrow-key navigation broken (focus_by_window staleness + fixture-only tests)'
+---
+## What
+The in-progress host-driven nav rewrite (uncommitted working tree as of 2026-06-06) **broke spatial navigation** ‚ÄĒ arrow keys no longer move focus. OS-menu progress is real, but the inline React nav path was removed before the host-driven path is reliable.
+
+This regression rides on the focus-source-of-truth seam, NOT the geometry pull (the on-demand geometry + lock discipline are implemented correctly).
+
+## What the rewrite did (context)
+- Removed `NAV_COMMAND_SPEC` / `buildNavCommands` / `buildDrillCommands` from `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx` (‚ąí273) ‚ÄĒ the inline arrow-key path that read the webview's live focus + built the snapshot per keystroke is **gone**.
+- Directional/drill `nav.*` are now host-driven plugin commands ‚Üí focus op `navigate focus` with `{ window }` only. `nav.jump` is a webview-bus handler.
+- Added F1 (`apps/kanban-app/src/ui_request.rs` + `ui-request-responder.ts`) and F2 (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/provider.rs` `UiGeometryProvider`, `TauriUiGeometryProvider`, `query geometry/scope_chain/focus` ops). Webview responder `registerUiResponder("focus.geometry", ‚Ä¶)` reads live `focusedFqRef.current` and rebuilds the snapshot on demand (no cache ‚ÄĒ correct).
+- `nav.yaml` + `nav_yaml.rs` deleted.
+
+## Root cause (two silent-drop points in `resolve_nav_source`, server.rs)
+1. **`from = focus_by_window[window]` is stale/empty ‚Üí "window has no focused slot ‚ÄĒ dropping."** The new path makes the KERNEL authoritative for current focus (`focus_by_window`), but the **frontend is the real source of truth** for focus (`FRONTEND_AUTHORITATIVE_KINDS = { scope_chain }` in `ui-state-context.tsx`; the kernel copy is a write-only echo). Any focus change that does not reliably push into the kernel's `focus_by_window` (click-to-focus especially) leaves the kernel navigating from a stale node or dropping.
+2. **kernel `from` vs webview snapshot divergence.** The kernel navigates from *its* `focus_by_window[window]`, but the snapshot is built around the *webview's* `focusedFqRef`. If they differ, `pick_target` can't find `from` in the snapshot ‚Üí drop/misbehave. Note the responder also exposes `focus.current`, but the op resolves `from` from the kernel map instead of using the webview's authoritative focus.
+3. **window-moniker dependency** ‚ÄĒ the host path needs the correct `{window}` from the dispatch scope chain; a missing `window:` moniker (see harden card `01KTECWA8D05FVKJ80MA3H0FFY`) ‚Üí wrong/empty window ‚Üí drop.
+
+## Why tests are green but the app broke
+`crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_nav_commands_e2e.rs` injects a fake `SeedProvider` (fixed snapshot) and the focus tests use `FakeProvider` ‚ÄĒ pre-seeded focus + guaranteed geometry. The real `TauriUiGeometryProvider` round-trip and `focus_by_window` freshness against real focus changes are **never exercised**. Fixture-only coverage: green on mocks, broken on the live path. (See [[feedback_fixture_only_anti_pattern]] / [[feedback_real_path_tests]].)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Arrow keys move focus again in the running app (board, grid, inspector) ‚ÄĒ directional + first/last + drill in/out.
+- [ ] One of: (a) prove `focus_by_window` tracks the webview's focus on EVERY focus change incl. click-to-focus (reliable push), OR (b) have the nav op resolve `from` from the provider's `focus.current` (webview-authoritative) so kernel and snapshot can't diverge.
+- [ ] The host path resolves the correct `{window}` from the dispatch scope chain (no default-to-main).
+- [ ] Root cause documented (focus-source-of-truth seam).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] A REAL-PATH test (NOT SeedProvider/FakeProvider): drive a focus change (incl. a click-to-focus) ‚Üí arrow key ‚Üí assert the focus actually moves to the geometrically-correct neighbor, exercising the live `focus_by_window` + provider path.
+- [ ] Regression test that fails on the current working tree (nav drops) and passes after the fix.
+- [ ] Existing spatial suite stays green.
+
+## Related
+- F2 / nav-server card `01KTEEDA9ZVTZ2R5CERW0WGK97` (this regression is its quality gate).
+- Card A `01KTCQFH7AEQDZD0QETSMCMGP0` (removed the inline path; can't be "done" while nav is broken).
+- F1 `01KTEFP1RMM2G65GR1PH9YQWEZ` (the host‚ÜíUI channel the pull rides on).
+- Harden `01KTECWA8D05FVKJ80MA3H0FFY` (window-moniker resolution ‚ÄĒ drop point #3).
\ No newline at end of file

+## ============ RUNTIME FINDINGS (2026-06-06, second session) ============
+Verified against the live app's unified log, in two stages.
+
+### Stage 1 fix applied (uncommitted): focus now resolves
+Edited `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/server.rs::resolve_nav_source` + `resolve_drill_source` to resolve `from` as wire ‚Üí **`provider.focus` (UI-authoritative)** ‚Üí kernel `focus_by_window` (fallback), and `state.rs::navigate` now reconciles the kernel slot to the resolved `from` (correct `prev_fq`). Added a REAL-condition test `navigate_pulls_focus_from_provider_when_kernel_slot_empty` (no seeded slot) in `tests/integration/ui_geometry_provider.rs`. Focus crate + command-service integration + kanban-app build all green. This implements acceptance (b).
+
+RESULT IN THE RUNNING APP: the log message changed from `window has no focused slot ‚ÄĒ dropping` ‚Üí `provider yielded no snapshot ‚ÄĒ dropping`. So **focus now resolves; the GEOMETRY pull is the remaining failure.**
+
+### Stage 2 root cause: the host‚ÜíUI round-trip races itself (NOT a timeout-length problem)
+`DEFAULT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT` is 5s, but `nav.down` completes in ~2.5ms returning `event:null` ‚ÄĒ so `request_from_ui` returns `Err` almost immediately, NOT after 5s. The log floods with:
+```
+ui_request_reply: no in-flight request for id (already timed out?)   ‚Üź every reply, 3-4√ó per request_id
+provider yielded no snapshot ‚ÄĒ dropping  op="navigate focus"
+```
+Diagnosis: the `request_from_ui` future is **dropped/cancelled before the webview reply arrives** (the awaiting `oneshot::Receiver` is gone), so `fulfill` finds the still-registered sender but `tx.send` fails ‚Üí logs "no in-flight (already timed out?)". The same `request_id` getting 3-4 replies means **multiple responders/windows answer one request** (duplicate `registerUiResponder` across provider instances, and/or 4 board windows). The nested host‚ÜíUI‚Üíhost round-trip runs MID-`dispatch_command`; the plugin‚Üíhost in-process MCP call (or the dispatch) appears to cancel/drop the nested request before the reply lands ‚ÄĒ the re-entrancy/cancellation hazard F1's exploration explicitly flagged.

(See [[feedback_fixture_only_anti_pattern]] / [[feedback_real_path_tests]].)
+So even with focus resolved (acceptance b done), the geometry pull cannot complete reliably while it is a nested round-trip inside the dispatch that triggered it.
+
+## ============ RECOMMENDATION (second session) ============
+Reconsider the architecture, don't just lengthen the timeout. The host-driven geometry PULL is fragile precisely because nav originates in the webview (where focus+geometry already live) and bounces host‚ÜíUI‚Üíhost. The **proven** path (the removed inline React nav) and the owner's own "**handler bus for ALL webview behaviors**" decision both point to:
+- Keep `nav.*` DEFINED in the `nav-commands` plugin (id/name/keys/menu) ‚Üí OS menu unchanged.
+- EXECUTE `nav.*` via the **webview command bus** (Card B), same as `nav.jump`: the handler reads live `focusedFqRef` + builds the snapshot + calls the focus op INLINE (the old `buildNavCommands`/`buildDrillCommands` logic, relocated into bus handlers keyed by the plugin command id). No host‚ÜíUI round-trip; definitions still live in the plugin.
+- This makes F1/F2 (`ui_request`, `UiGeometryProvider`) optional for nav ‚ÄĒ keep them for genuine host-originated needs (agents/automation), but nav itself stops depending on the fragile mid-dispatch pull.

-- [ ] One of: (a) prove `focus_by_window` tracks the webview's focus on EVERY focus change incl. click-to-focus (reliable push), OR (b) have the nav op resolve `from` from the provider's `focus.current` (webview-authoritative) so kernel and snapshot can't diverge.

-- [ ] Root cause documented (focus-source-of-truth seam).
+- [ ] Arrow keys move focus again in the running app (board, grid, inspector) ‚ÄĒ directional + first/last + drill in/out + Escape.
+- [ ] Decide + implement: (A) make the host‚ÜíUI round-trip reliable mid-dispatch (fix the future-cancellation + duplicate-responder races), OR (B) execute nav via the webview bus inline (recommended above) ‚ÄĒ definitions stay in the plugin either way.
+- [ ] The host path (if kept) resolves the correct `{window}` from the dispatch scope chain (no default-to-main).
+- [ ] Root cause documented (done above).

-- [ ] Regression test that fails on the current working tree (nav drops) and passes after the fix.
